title: leucanthemum atratum subsp. corsicum
intro: Leucanthemum atratum subsp. corsicum, also known as the Corsican Shasta Daisy or Corsican Oxeye Daisy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Leucanthemum atratum subsp. corsicum is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
